description: Returns a random number in the range [0, 1)—including 0, excluding 1—each time it is evaluated. Use for A/B splits, sampling, or any logic that needs a random value. Optionally configure min and max for a different range when the API supports it.

when_to_use: A/B testing or experimentation (e.g. assign random bucket 0–1 for variant selection)
Sampling a percentage of users or events
Randomizing order or selection in tags
Any condition or tag that should behave randomly per evaluation
common_use_cases: A/B test: compare variable to 0.5 for 50/50 split, or use buckets for multiple variants
Sampling: fire tag only when random < 0.1 for 10% of page views
Random IDs or seeds derived from the value
Load balancing or random choice between options
best_practices: Default range is [0, 1); no configuration required for that behavior
Value is re-evaluated each time the variable is used
Use in triggers or tags that need per-page or per-event randomness

create_variable.description: Create a random number variable that returns a value in [0, 1) by default

variable_type.description: Must be 'random' for random number variables

update_example.description: Update the name of an existing random variable

with_range_example.description: Create random variable with optional min/max (if API supports)

common_mistakes: Expecting the same value on every evaluation—the value changes each time the variable is read
Assuming range other than [0, 1) without confirming API support for min_value/max_value

always_same_value.problem: Variable seems to return the same value
solutions: Random is evaluated per use; ensure you are reading the variable in the context where it is evaluated (e.g. tag or trigger)
Check that you are not reusing a cached value from a previous request or page
